div1.entryFree: Wane, vb. to decrease; to decline: in --ing age, Lucr. 142. Shr. Ind. 2, 65. II, 403. as fast as thou shalt w., so fast thou growest in one of thine, Sonn. 11, 1. who hast by --ing grown, and therein showest thy lovers withering as thy sweet self growest, 126, 3. how slow this old moon --s, Mids. I, 1, 4. I seek not to wax great by others' --ing, H6B IV, 10, 22 (O. Edd. warning. I shall interche ge my --d state for Henry's regal crown, H6C IV, 7, 4. to watch the --ing of my adversaries, R3 IV, 4, 4. In Ant. II, 1, 21 O. Edd. thy wand lip; M. Edd. waned; perhaps wanned. cf. Beauty-waning.
